questions.q: Who was the founder of the Carolingian dynasty?
What was the significance of the Carolingian Renaissance?
questions.answer: The Carolingian Renaissance was a period of cultural, intellectual and artistic revival in Europe during the 8th and 9th centuries.

questions.q: The social hierarchy of the Carolingian society was primarily based on which of the following?
What was the role of the nobility in the Carolingian society?
questions.answer: The nobility played a crucial role in the Carolingian society as they held the highest positions of power and authority. They were responsible for maintaining law and order, collecting taxes, and fighting wars on behalf of the king.

questions.q: The Carolingian Empire was a loose network of _______________________ under the authority of the king.
The Carolingian Renaissance had a significant impact on the _______________________ of Europe.
The Frankish monarchy was characterized by a strong _______________________ of the king.
The Carolingian society was divided into three orders of society. What were these orders?
questions.answer: The three orders of society were the Clergy, the Nobility, and the Commoners.

questions.q: The Carolingian Renaissance was a period of _______________________ in Europe during the 8th and 9th centuries.
The Carolingian Empire was characterized by a system of _______________________ where the king held the highest authority.
